tests.test_body:
target datalayout = "e-m:e-p:32:32-p270:32:32-p271:32:32-p272:64:64-f64:32:64-f80:32-n8:16:32-S128"
target triple = "i386-unknown-linux-gnu"

declare ptr @foo()

declare i32 @memcmp(ptr inreg nocapture noundef, ptr inreg nocapture noundef, i32 inreg noundef)

define i32 @baz(ptr inreg noundef %s2, i32 inreg noundef %n) {

issue.title: [instcombine] dropped inreg function argument attribute
issue.body:
[inreg langref](https://llvm.org/docs/LangRef.html#parameter-attributes)

When instcombine replace calls to `memcmp` with `bcmp`, it happens to NOT carry over the `inreg` flags on the arguments when they exist.  `inreg` is set by `-mregparm=3` for `-m32` (32b x86) linux kernel builds.

blocks #4440

example: https://godbolt.org/z/z3fjveP4h

This is creating fun crashes at boot time because we've now totally messed up the calling convention.
issue.author: nickdesaulniers

comments.author: nickdesaulniers
comments.body: `emitLibCall` in llvm/lib/Transforms/Utils/BuildLibCalls.cpp potentially should be copying over the `AttributeSet` from the previous call to the newly synthesized call. Or at least just the `AttributeList::getParamAttrs`.

comments.author: efriedma-quic
comments.body: If all the utilities in BuildLibCalls.h are broken, we need a general fix for all of them.  Copying the attributes from another call isn't going to work in general; we don't have a call with the relevant attributes from in some cases.  I guess we need something equivalent to X86TargetLowering::markLibCallAttributes.

comments.author: nickdesaulniers
efriedma-quic
comments.body: The correct number of "inreg" markings for a call to bcmp should be computed based on its signature and the module flag NumRegisterParameters.  Anything else is irrelevant, including the inputs to the call, or any aspect of the call it's replacing.

comments.author: nickdesaulniers
comments.body:
> Copying the attributes from another call isn't going to work in general

Perhaps, can you think of a case where we should _not_ be reusing the existing [param attrs](https://llvm.org/docs/LangRef.html#parameter-attributes)?

I think it would be better to sanitize certain param attributes (i.e. remove them) if they exist, rather than add specific checks for copying over specific flags.

> we don't have a call with the relevant attributes from in some cases.

That's ok, that's what the `dyn_cast<Argument>` in my above diff will find out.  That cast will fail for newly constructed params.  I think it's more important though that parameters with existing attributes don't have their attributes dropped simply because we created a call to a different function.

In [my godbolt link above](https://godbolt.org/z/z3fjveP4h), we're not just dropping `inreg`, but also `noundef` param attributes.

(cc @davidbolvansky )

comments.author: davidbolvansky
comments.body: As inreg is not a "hint" which can be dropped, maybe just create list of attributes which cannot be dropped as they are important? Then just skip SimplifyLibCalls pass for such calls with those attributes? And yes, .. current managment of call attributes in SLC is not ideal.

comments.author: nickdesaulniers
comments.body:
> The correct number of "inreg" markings for a call to bcmp should be computed based on its signature and the module flag NumRegisterParameters. Anything else is irrelevant

> As inreg is not a "hint" which can be dropped

Perhaps `X86TargetLowering::markLibCallAttributes` isn't being called when it should be. I don't see `SelectionDAGLegalize::ExpandLibCall` or `X86TargetLowering::markLibCallAttributes` called at all for [my test case](https://godbolt.org/z/z3fjveP4h).

comments.author: efriedma-quic
nickdesaulniers
comments.body:
Is https://reviews.llvm.org/rG0f8c626723d2 how I should be solving this?

cc @JonPsson @nikic

comments.author: efriedma-quic
comments.body: On its own, I'd say fine.  But I'm not sure about cherry-picking 0f8c6267

comments.author: nickdesaulniers
comments.body: yeah, ok, np, this one can ride the trains.
